# Plan: Baoh Assistant - Single Follow-Up Round (10 Questions Max)

> Source PRD: Grilling-session decisions from 2026-03-31

## Status

- Completed through Phase 4 (implemented and validated on 2026-03-31)

## Architectural decisions

Durable decisions that apply across all phases:

- Routes: no route changes; existing multi-agent handoff remains triage -> introduction -> discovery -> contact_collection -> summariser -> email
- Schema: no schema migration in this slice; keep existing presale lead columns
- Key models: preserve `PresaleLead`, `AskedQuestion`, and `DiscoveryQuestion` model shapes
- Follow-up limits: initial questions capped at 5, follow-up questions capped at 5, follow-up rounds capped at 1
- Completion gates: move to contact collection when (a) required fields are complete and explicit confirmation is received, or (b) follow-up limits are exhausted (`CurrentDiscoveryRound >= 1` and `FollowUpAskedCount >= 5`)
- Anti-stall thresholds: soft-disabled in transition logic; counters remain persisted for backward compatibility

---

## Phase 1: Enforce One Follow-Up Round

User stories covered: US-1, US-2, US-3

### What to build

Reduce the follow-up round budget from 2 to 1 in deterministic policy and transition services. Keep the 5 follow-up question cap and update follow-up exhaustion checks to the one-round model. Preserve early completion with explicit confirmation.

### Acceptance criteria

- [x] Follow-up round default is 1 in follow-up policy and transition requests
- [x] Discovery transitions when `CurrentDiscoveryRound >= 1` and `FollowUpAskedCount >= 5`
- [x] Early confirmation path still transitions to contact collection

---

## Phase 2: Make Follow-Up Batch Reach 5 Questions

User stories covered: US-1, US-2

### What to build

Update follow-up selection to ask exactly five follow-up questions when the follow-up budget allows five. Prioritize unresolved critical topics first, then fill remaining slots from valid supplemental candidates with valid initial-parent linkage and deduplicated topic keys.

### Acceptance criteria

- [x] With follow-up budget of 5 and at least 5 valid proposed candidates, exactly 5 are approved
- [x] Approved follow-ups remain linked to initial parent asked questions
- [x] Unresolved critical topics are prioritized ahead of supplemental questions

---

## Phase 3: Update Agent Prompt Contract

User stories covered: US-1, US-4

### What to build

Update discovery and triage prompt instructions so they explicitly reflect one follow-up round and one-round exhaustion routing. Remove anti-stall fallback wording from prompt guidance and keep deterministic transition-tool instructions.

### Acceptance criteria

- [x] Prompt text states follow-up rounds cap is 1
- [x] Triage routing checks one-round exhaustion for contact-collection handoff
- [x] Prompt-contract tests validate one-round language

---

## Phase 4: Regression and Scenario Tests

User stories covered: US-1, US-2, US-3, US-4

### What to build

Update focused tests to validate one-round behavior, disabled anti-stall threshold transitions, and deterministic follow-up approval under a five-question budget.

### Acceptance criteria

- [x] Discovery transition tests pass with anti-stall thresholds no longer forcing fallback
- [x] Follow-up policy tests pass with one-round expectations
- [x] Follow-up persistence tests pass with round number 1
- [x] Prompt contract tests pass with one-round wording

---

## Implementation notes (2026-03-31)

- Added deterministic supplemental follow-up pool in policy evaluator to fill the follow-up batch to budget when LLM proposes too few valid items.
- Supplemental candidates enforce valid initial-parent linkage and topic deduplication.
- Added regression coverage for deterministic supplemental fill behavior and updated affected policy tests.
- Validation completed with `dotnet test tests/InterviewCoach.Agent.Tests/InterviewCoach.Agent.Tests.csproj` (46 passed).
